root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky under the race detector because it executed DDL statements from a goroutine using shared session/testkit state, leading to unsynchronized access to session internals. This PR stabilizes it by executing the DDL in the goroutine via a separate session (e.g., `sessionExec`) instead of reusing the shared test helper.
fix_pattern: Avoid sharing a session/testkit across goroutines; run async DDL via a separate session/connection (or synchronize access) to prevent -race data races.
